areas._note: count = directory INDEX entries (data/areas.json), built from data/source/villages.csv (192 villages). The per-area files (data/areas/*.json) AND the Supabase areas mirror are 196: the 4 extra are deactivated duplicate-Rightmove / merged variants intentionally kept out of the index — charlwood-so24, colemore-gu32, flexcombe-gu33, froxfield-green-gu32 (flexcombe-gu33 research merged into the canonical flexcombe-gu32). Guarded by tests/areas-index-sync.test.js. (Corrected 2026-06-03: the prior note's gu34 ids were stale.) 2026-06-03: 100 areas down-synced from Supabase (DB was source for this run); repo now 192 researched + 1 partial (lane-end-so21) + 3 directory (ebble-sp5, newtown-sp5, worthy-down-so21 — not yet researched in Supabase). 2026-06-03: added whiteley-po15 (researched). 2026-06-04: chillworth-so16 set active:false (soft-ignored — pruned from the fetcher's L7.5 active set; still in index/mirror); its 277 listings rejected. 2026-06-04: hambledon-po7 likewise set active:false; its 244 listings rejected (212 new wrong_area). 2026-06-04: soft-ignored 6 more areas (active:false) — newton-stacey-sp11, wherwell-sp11, bordon-gu35, sherrington-ba12, tisbury-sp3, southwick-po17. All 8 removed areas' listings (969 total) re-tagged reject/removed_area — a new administrative reason EXCLUDED from preference learning (see assets/js/listings/reactions.js SYSTEM_REJECT_REASONS + learned-preferences weights.js isTraining), so wholesale area ignores no longer skew learned type/outcode/price weights.

listing_reactions._note: v3 L3: append-only reaction log (like/pass/reject + reason, listing_snapshot). 1136 rows as of 2026-06-04 (264 on 2026-06-03; 0 at table creation 2026-05-30). 2026-06-04: bulk reject batches via MCP — unwanted property types (Flat/Apartment/Penthouse→wrong_house_type/flat, plus Maisonette/Ground Flat/Ground Maisonette/Retirement/Coach House/Park Home/Land/Plot/Mobile Home/House Share/HMO/Garages/Farm Land/Equestrian/Not Specified→reject) and all chillworth-so16 listings→reject/wrong_area. 2026-06-04: PRICE-BAND batch — 265 active listings priced >£440k bulk-rejected reject/too_expensive (reasons sub-detail over_budget → price-band training signal), with snapshots so weights.js#isTraining keeps them; the 1 over-£440k liked listing preserved (§18.5). Active pool 945→680. Append-only — Supabase always wins; snapshot pulled live via MCP, never written back by Claude. Tracked sync table #18 (16 user-state + 2 content).

learned_preferences._note: v3 L4/L5: distilled preference weights, one row/household (derived = Layer 2 from the reaction log; overrides = Layer 3 manual/AI; dismissals = L5 conflict-prompt quiet-until map, added by migration learned_preferences_dismissals_l5). 1 row present, recomputed from the reaction log as of 2026-06-03 (0 at table creation 2026-05-31). Recomputed user-state — Supabase wins; snapshot pulled live via MCP. Tracked sync table #19 (17 user-state + 2 content).

area_confirmations._note: v3 Step5: per-household area location review confirmations blob ({ confirmed: { areaId: isoTimestamp } }), one row/household. 1 row present (count was previously 191 — a copy-paste error from the areas block, corrected 2026-06-03 against live via MCP). Tracked sync table #20 (18 user-state + 2 content).

listings._note: v3 L1: fetcher-written live content (NOT git-versioned, NOT one of the 20 tracked git-synced tables). Backfilled 2026-05-31 with 1298 real listings via tools/import-apify-runs.mjs (storage read of already-paid Apify datasets, no new spend) across 12 outcodes; 0 unmatched, 0 out-of-region. Fetcher BASELINE_PRICE_MAX lowered 500k→450k so over-budget listings stop arriving. 2026-06-04 P5 junk purge (Supabase MCP, user-approved): DELETEd 1,671 baseline-violating rows that were NOT liked (excluded type / >£450k / <£100k / <2 beds); 3,086→1,415, feed-visible 2,539→1,252, 0 baseline-violators remain. The reject SIGNAL persists in the append-only listing_reactions log (unchanged at 3,244) so feed suppression survives the purge; all 20 liked rows preserved. Forward maintenance now via tools/purge-listings.mjs.
